title: The Square Root of 2 is Irrational

md: Some numbers are **rational** — they can be written as a fraction $\frac{p}{q}$ of two whole numbers. Examples: $\frac{3}{4}$, $7 = \frac{7}{1}$, even $0.25 = \frac{1}{4}$.
The ancient Greeks assumed *every* number worked this way. Then they met $\sqrt{2}$ — the length of the diagonal of a unit square — and it broke their world. This proof shows why.

blocks.title: The claim
blocks.body: There is **no** fraction $\frac{p}{q}$ of whole numbers whose square is $2$. We say $\sqrt{2}$ is *irrational*.

md: We can't check every fraction — there are infinitely many. So instead we *assume the opposite* of what we want, and follow the logic until it collapses into something impossible. If the assumption forces a contradiction, the assumption must have been false.

**Step 1 — Assume $\sqrt{2}$ *is* rational.** Then we can write it as a fraction. And we can always reduce a fraction to **lowest terms** — so assume $p$ and $q$ share no common factor. In particular, $p$ and $q$ are *not both even* (or we could cancel a 2).

blocks.tex: \sqrt{2} = \frac{p}{q}, \qquad p, q \text{ share no common factor}

md: **Step 2 — Square both sides** and clear the denominator:
**Step 3 — So $p^2$ is even.** It equals $2$ times something. But here is the key fact about even and odd numbers:
- so if $p$ were **odd**, then $p^2$ would be **odd**.
Since $p^2$ is even, $p$ itself must be **even**. Write $p = 2k$.
**Step 5 — So $q^2$ is even too** — which by the same reasoning as Step 3 means **$q$ is even**.

blocks.title: The contradiction
blocks.body: We just proved $p$ is even **and** $q$ is even. But in Step 1 we said they share no common factor! Both being even means both are divisible by $2$. That is impossible — the assumption has destroyed itself.

md: Since assuming "$\sqrt{2}$ is rational" leads to an impossibility, that assumption is **false**. Therefore:

blocks.title: Why this proof matters
blocks.body: The same argument works for $\sqrt{3}$, $\sqrt{5}$, $\sqrt{6}$ — any whole number that isn't a perfect square. It was one of the first times humans proved something *cannot* exist, using nothing but logic.
